name: US Presidents in Order
description: All US presidents in order -- number, name, party, and key facts

questions.question: Who was the 1st President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George Washington (1789-1797) was the only president elected unanimously by the Electoral College -- twice. He set the precedent of a two-term limit 150 years before it became law.

questions.question: Who was the 2nd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: John Adams (Federalist, 1797-1801) was the first president to live in the White House. He and Thomas Jefferson both died on July 4, 1826 -- the 50th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.

questions.question: Who was the 3rd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Thomas Jefferson (Democratic-Republican, 1801-1809) doubled the size of the United States with the Louisiana Purchase for about 3 cents per acre. He also authored the Declaration of Independence.

questions.question: Who was the 4th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: James Madison (Democratic-Republican, 1809-1817) is called the 'Father of the Constitution.' At 5'4" and barely 100 pounds, he was the smallest president in American history.

questions.question: Who was the 5th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: James Monroe (Democratic-Republican, 1817-1825) issued the Monroe Doctrine, warning Europe to stay out of the Western Hemisphere. His presidency was called the 'Era of Good Feelings' because there was effectively only one political party.

questions.question: Who was the 6th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: John Quincy Adams (Democratic-Republican, 1825-1829) was the first son of a president to become president. After losing re-election, he served 17 years in the House of Representatives -- the only ex-president to do so.

questions.question: Who was the 7th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Andrew Jackson (Democrat, 1829-1837) was the first 'populist' president, championing the common man against elites. He survived the first presidential assassination attempt when both of the attacker's pistols misfired.

questions.question: Who was the 8th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Martin Van Buren (Democrat, 1837-1841) was the first president born as a US citizen rather than a British subject. He grew up speaking Dutch and English was his second language.

questions.question: Who was the 9th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: William Henry Harrison (Whig, 1841) delivered the longest inaugural address in history -- in the rain, without a coat -- and died of pneumonia 31 days later, making his the shortest presidency ever.

questions.question: Who was the 10th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: John Tyler (Whig, 1841-1845) was the first VP to assume the presidency after a death. His entire cabinet resigned in protest and his own party expelled him, earning him the nickname 'His Accidency.'

questions.question: Who was the 11th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: James K. Polk (Democrat, 1845-1849) is considered one of the most effective one-term presidents. He set four major goals -- including acquiring California and settling the Oregon boundary -- achieved all four, and declined to run again.

questions.question: Who was the 12th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Zachary Taylor (Whig, 1849-1850) was a war hero who had never voted before running for president. He died 16 months into office, possibly from gastroenteritis after eating cherries and drinking iced milk at a July 4th celebration.

questions.question: Who was the 13th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Millard Fillmore (Whig, 1850-1853) rose from extreme poverty in a log cabin to the presidency. He signed the Compromise of 1850, which delayed the Civil War but included the reviled Fugitive Slave Act.

questions.question: Who was the 14th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Franklin Pierce (Democrat, 1853-1857) saw his 11-year-old son killed in a train wreck two months before his inauguration. His presidency was haunted by personal grief and the deepening national crisis over slavery.

questions.question: Who was the 15th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: James Buchanan (Democrat, 1857-1861) is the only president who never married. He is widely ranked among the worst presidents for failing to prevent the secession crisis that led to the Civil War.

questions.question: Who was the 16th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Abraham Lincoln (Republican, 1861-1865) preserved the Union through the Civil War and ended slavery with the Emancipation Proclamation. He was assassinated by John Wilkes Booth at Ford's Theatre, just days after the war ended.

questions.question: Who was the 17th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Andrew Johnson (National Union/Democrat, 1865-1869) was the first president to be impeached, surviving removal by a single Senate vote. A self-taught tailor, he didn't learn to read until his wife taught him as an adult.

questions.question: Who was the 18th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Ulysses S. Grant (Republican, 1869-1877) was the Union's commanding general before becoming president. His real name was Hiram Ulysses Grant -- the 'S' came from a clerical error at West Point that he decided to keep.

questions.question: Who was the 19th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Rutherford B. Hayes (Republican, 1877-1881) won the most disputed election in US history, decided by a single electoral vote after a congressional commission. Critics called him 'Rutherfraud' and 'His Fraudulency.'

questions.question: Who was the 20th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: James A. Garfield (Republican, 1881) was the last president born in a log cabin. He was shot by a disgruntled office-seeker just four months into his term and died 11 weeks later -- doctors probing for the bullet likely worsened the infection that killed him.

questions.question: Who was the 21st President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Chester A. Arthur (Republican, 1881-1885) was a surprise reformer: despite being a product of the spoils system, he signed the Pendleton Civil Service Act, creating merit-based federal hiring. He was known as 'Elegant Arthur' for his love of fine clothes.

questions.question: Who was the 22nd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Grover Cleveland (Democrat, 1885-1889) is the only president to serve two non-consecutive terms (#22 and #24). In his first term, he was the first Democrat elected after the Civil War and the first president married in the White House.

questions.question: Who was the 23rd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Benjamin Harrison (Republican, 1889-1893) was the grandson of President William Henry Harrison (#9), making them the only grandfather-grandson presidential pair. He won the Electoral College despite losing the popular vote to Cleveland.

questions.question: Who was the 24th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Grover Cleveland (Democrat, 1893-1897) returned to defeat Benjamin Harrison in a rematch, becoming the only president to serve non-consecutive terms (#22 and #24). During his second term, he secretly had surgery for oral cancer on a yacht to avoid public panic.

questions.question: Who was the 25th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: William McKinley (Republican, 1897-1901) led the US to victory in the Spanish-American War, making it an imperial power with territories from Puerto Rico to the Philippines. He was assassinated by an anarchist at the Pan-American Exposition in Buffalo.

questions.question: Who was the 26th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Theodore Roosevelt (Republican, 1901-1909) became president at 42, the youngest ever. A relentless force of nature, he busted trusts, built the Panama Canal, and once finished a campaign speech after being shot in the chest -- the bullet slowed by his folded speech and glasses case.

questions.question: Who was the 27th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: William Howard Taft (Republican, 1909-1913) weighed over 300 pounds and allegedly got stuck in the White House bathtub. He is the only person to serve as both president and Chief Justice of the Supreme Court, and he preferred the latter.

questions.question: Who was the 28th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Woodrow Wilson (Democrat, 1913-1921) led America through World War I and championed the League of Nations. After a severe stroke in 1919, his wife Edith effectively ran the executive branch in secret for the remainder of his term.

questions.question: Who was the 29th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Warren G. Harding (Republican, 1921-1923) won in a landslide promising a 'return to normalcy' after WWI. He died in office, and his presidency became synonymous with scandal -- the Teapot Dome affair was the Watergate of its era.

questions.question: Who was the 30th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Calvin Coolidge (Republican, 1923-1929) was so famously silent that he was nicknamed 'Silent Cal.' When a dinner guest told him she had bet she could get him to say more than two words, he replied: 'You lose.'

questions.question: Who was the 31st President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Herbert Hoover (Republican, 1929-1933) was a self-made millionaire and brilliant humanitarian who fed starving Europeans after WWI. But the Great Depression struck eight months into his term, and the shantytowns of the homeless became known as 'Hoovervilles.'

questions.question: Who was the 32nd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Franklin D. Roosevelt (Democrat, 1933-1945) is the only president elected four times. He led America through the Great Depression and most of World War II -- all while using a wheelchair, which the press agreed never to photograph.

questions.question: Who was the 33rd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Harry S. Truman (Democrat, 1945-1953) made the decision to drop atomic bombs on Japan, ending WWII. The 'S' in his name doesn't stand for anything -- it honored both grandfathers. He left office with dismal approval ratings but is now considered a near-great president.

questions.question: Who was the 34th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Dwight D. Eisenhower (Republican, 1953-1961) was the Supreme Allied Commander in WWII before becoming president. He built the Interstate Highway System, which was originally justified as a military defense project, and warned of the 'military-industrial complex' in his farewell address.

questions.question: Who was the 35th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: John F. Kennedy (Democrat, 1961-1963) was the youngest elected president at 43 and the first Catholic to hold the office. His assassination in Dallas on November 22, 1963, remains one of the most analyzed events in American history.

questions.question: Who was the 36th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Lyndon B. Johnson (Democrat, 1963-1969) passed the Civil Rights Act and Medicare as part of his 'Great Society,' but the Vietnam War destroyed his presidency. He was sworn in on Air Force One beside a blood-stained Jackie Kennedy.

questions.question: Who was the 37th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Richard Nixon (Republican, 1969-1974) opened diplomatic relations with China and created the EPA, but Watergate made his name synonymous with political scandal. He is the only president to resign from office.

questions.question: Who was the 38th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Gerald Ford (Republican, 1974-1977) is the only president never elected as either president or vice president -- he was appointed VP after Agnew resigned, then became president when Nixon resigned. His pardon of Nixon was hugely controversial but later won him a Profile in Courage award.

questions.question: Who was the 39th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Jimmy Carter (Democrat, 1977-1981) brokered the Camp David Accords between Israel and Egypt, but the Iran hostage crisis dominated his final year. His post-presidency humanitarian work earned him the 2002 Nobel Peace Prize, and he lived to be 100 years old.

questions.question: Who was the 40th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Ronald Reagan (Republican, 1981-1989) was a Hollywood actor turned politician who became the face of modern conservatism. He survived an assassination attempt just 69 days into office, quipping to surgeons: 'I hope you're all Republicans.'

questions.question: Who was the 41st President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George H. W. Bush (Republican, 1989-1993) led the US through the end of the Cold War and the Gulf War. A WWII Navy pilot shot down over the Pacific at age 20, he was one of the most experienced people ever to hold the presidency.

questions.question: Who was the 42nd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Bill Clinton (Democrat, 1993-2001) presided over the longest peacetime economic expansion in US history and left office with a budget surplus. He was the second president impeached by the House but was acquitted by the Senate.

questions.question: Who was the 43rd President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George W. Bush (Republican, 2001-2009) won one of the closest elections in history, decided by the Supreme Court and 537 votes in Florida. The 9/11 attacks defined his presidency, leading to wars in Afghanistan and Iraq.

questions.question: Who was the 44th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Barack Obama (Democrat, 2009-2017) was the first African American president, winning with the slogan 'Yes We Can.' He signed the Affordable Care Act into law and ordered the raid that killed Osama bin Laden.

questions.question: Who was the 45th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Donald Trump (Republican, 2017-2021) was the first president with no prior political or military experience, coming from business and reality TV. He was impeached twice by the House but acquitted both times by the Senate.

questions.question: Who was the 46th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Joe Biden (Democrat, 2021-2025) was elected at 78, making him the oldest person to assume the presidency. He previously served 36 years as a senator from Delaware and 8 years as Obama's vice president.

questions.question: Who is the 47th President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Donald Trump (Republican, 2025-present) became the second president to serve non-consecutive terms, after Grover Cleveland. He is the first president in over a century to win back the White House after losing re-election.
